Title: Sirāt
Author: Unknown
Summary: "Sirāt" is an R-rated drama directed by Oliver Laxe, set in a suspense genre. The 115-minute film, starring Sergi López and others, follows a father and son searching for their missing daughter at a Moroccan rave. It releases in Los Angeles and New York on November 14, 2025, and nationwide on March 6, 2026.
Content:
Sirāt is a drama film set in a foreign suspense genre, rated R, with a runtime of 115 minutes. It is scheduled for release on November 14, 2025, in Los Angeles and New York, and on March 6, 2026. Directed by Oliver Laxe and written by Santiago Fillol and Oliver Laxe, the film stars Sergi López, Bruno Núñez, Stefania Gadda, Joshua Liam Henderson, Tonin Janvier, Jade Oukid, and Richard Bellamy. 

The story follows a father and his son who arrive at a rave deep in the mountains of southern Morocco. They are searching for Mar, their daughter and sister, who vanished months earlier at one of these endless, sleepless parties. Surrounded by electronic music and a raw, unfamiliar sense of freedom, they repeatedly show her photo, holding onto hope as they follow a group of ravers to one last destination.

Title: The Bride
Author: Unknown
Summary: "The Bride" (2026), directed by Maggie Gyllenhaal, is an R-rated horror drama romance set for release on March 6, 2026. Featuring stars like Christian Bale, Jessie Buckley, and Penélope Cruz, the film follows a Frankenstein-like creation in 1930s Chicago, exploring themes of life, love, and macabre transformation. Warner Brothers produces the 126-minute film.
Content:
The Bride (2026) is a horror drama romance film rated R with a runtime of 126 minutes, set to be released on March 6, 2026. It is directed and written by Maggie Gyllenhaal, known for her work on The Lost Daughter. The cast includes Jessie Buckley, Christian Bale, Peter Sarsgaard, Annette Bening, Jake Gyllenhaal, and Penélope Cruz. The film is produced by Warner Brothers.

The story centers on a lonely Frankenstein (Bale) who travels to 1930s Chicago to meet groundbreaking scientist Dr. Euphronious (Annette Bening). Together, they revive a murdered young woman, resulting in the creation of The Bride (Buckley). The film offers a bold, iconoclastic take on a compelling story, blending life, macabre romance, and ambitious ideas. Maggie Gyllenhaal’s vision presents a beautiful yet hideous creature that is both captivating and unsettling.

Title: Hoppers
Author: Unknown
Summary: "Hoppers" is a 2026 animated sci-fi comedy film directed by Daniel Chong, set for release on March 6, 2026. Produced by Walt Disney, it stars Jon Hamm, Bobby Moynihan, and Piper Curda. The story involves scientists developing technology to transfer human consciousness into robotic animals, enabling communication with animals, with Mabel uncovering hidden mysteries.
Content:
Hoppers (2026) is an action animated comedy sci-fi film rated PG, with a runtime of 105 minutes. It is scheduled for release on March 6, 2026, and is directed by Daniel Chong, with writers Jesse Andrews and Daniel Chong. The cast includes Jon Hamm, Bobby Moynihan, and Piper Curda. The film is produced by Walt Disney Pictures.

In "Hoppers," scientists have discovered how to "hop" human consciousness into lifelike robotic animals, allowing people to communicate with animals as if they were animals. Using this new technology, Mabel (Curda) will uncover mysteries within the animal world that are beyond anything she could have imagined.

Title: How to Make a Killing
Author: Unknown
Summary: "How to Make a Killing" (2026) is an R-rated crime comedy directed by John Patton Ford, starring Glen Powell as Becket Redfellow, who seeks to inherit $28 billion by eliminating family members after being disowned at birth. The film features Margaret Qualley, Jessica Henwick, Ed Harris, and others, produced by A24.
Content:
How to Make a Killing (2026) is a crime suspense comedy rated R, with a runtime of 105 minutes. It is directed by John Patton Ford, who also wrote the screenplay. The cast includes Glen Powell, Margaret Qualley, Jessica Henwick, Bill Camp, Zach Woods, Topher Grace, and Ed Harris. The film is produced by A24.

The story centers on Becket Redfellow, played by Glen Powell, who was disowned at birth by his wealthy family. Determined to reclaim his inheritance, he is willing to go to any lengths, including eliminating relatives who stand in his way.

In the film, Powell's character is depicted as someone who has not been particularly fortunate in life—despite being attractive, successful, and surrounded by admirers. His ultimate goal is to inherit $28 billion by removing his family members.

Title: Traffic Collison - Circle Dr E/Highway 11 - Update
Author: Unknown
Summary: Saskatoon police report that one eastbound lane on Circle Drive and one northbound lane on Highway 11 are now open after a semi-trailer struck the bridge, causing damage. No injuries occurred. Traffic restrictions remain, and motorists are advised to use alternate routes as engineers assess the damage. The collision is under investigation.
Content:
The Saskatoon Police Service advises that city engineers have assessed the damage, and one eastbound lane on Circle Drive and one northbound lane of Highway 11 have been opened for traffic to proceed. The Collision Analyst Unit, in cooperation with the Saskatchewan Highway Patrol, is investigating the collision. No injuries were reported.

Traffic flow restrictions will remain until further notice. Motorists are asked to take alternate routes or expect delays in the area.

A semi with a trailer carrying equipment traveling northbound on Highway 11 from Regina, entering the city limits at the Circle Drive overpass, was too high for the structure and caused significant damage. The northbound lanes of Highway 11 into the city and the eastbound lanes of Circle Drive over the bridge will be closed until engineers from the City of Saskatoon assess the damage and determine if it is safe for travel. Motorists are advised to take alternate routes until further notice.

Title: The Inertia v3 Beta is Here
Author: Paul Redmond
Summary: The Inertia v3 Beta introduces a built-in XHR HTTP client, optional Axios support, and a new use Httphook for external HTTP requests with validation error handling. It adds optimistic updates across the router, use Form, and use Http, with support for concurrent updates and automatic rollback. Instant navigation and server-side URL fragment preservation are also included.
Content:
The Inertia v3 Beta is a major pre-release milestone that introduces several new features and improvements. It now includes a built-in XHR HTTP client, making Axios an optional peer dependency. Applications that do not require Axios can omit it entirely, while those that prefer it can continue using it by installing it as a peer dependency.

A new use Httphook allows making HTTP requests outside of Inertia, providing the same developer experience as use Form— including processing, errors, progress, and dirty state—but returns response data instead of triggering page navigation. It supports surfacing all validation errors, not just the first.

Optimistic updates have been added across the router, use Form, and use Http, including support for concurrent optimistic updates. The update callback can receive current props and return the optimistic state to apply immediately, with automatic rollback on validation errors, server errors, or interrupted requests. The router supports both a fluent API and an inline option for optimistic updates.

The use Form hook now works similarly, allowing optimistic updates when adding new items. The <Form> component also gains an optimistic prop for declarative handling.

Instant support has been added to make navigation feel immediate by rendering the destination page before the server response completes.

A new server-side preserve Fragment option keeps the URL hash fragment intact across redirects. In Laravel, chaining preserve Fragment() on a redirect response ensures the URL fragment is preserved even when the server includes preserve Fragment: true in the Inertia page object.

Title: He Had a Purple Heart, PTSD and a Rap Sheet. He Had to Leave the U.S.
Author: Max Kim
Summary: Sae Joon Park, a U.S. Army veteran deported last year, faces potential deafness in South Korea after a ruptured eardrum and worsening hearing loss. Having lived in the U.S. since age 7 and holding a Purple Heart, he struggles with language barriers and his criminal past, which led to his deportation. He has sought media attention and congressional help for his case.
Content:
Sae Joon Park’s life was in pieces after he was swept up in the Trump administration’s deportation push last year. But on a recent Tuesday, he was preoccupied with the more immediate concern that he might be going deaf.

At a walk-in clinic in Busan, South Korea, he explained to the receptionist, in slightly accented Korean, how his left eardrum had inexplicably ruptured in his sleep a few weeks earlier. Now his right ear was fading out, too.

“Fill in the blanks, please,” she said, producing an intake form.

“I can’t read or write Korean,” Mr. Park said.

It was a line he had delivered many times in recent weeks, explained by the fact that he hadn’t lived in South Korea since he was 7, when he immigrated to the United States. A U.S. Army veteran with combat trauma and a Purple Heart, he was ordered to leave America at the age of 56 over a criminal history that he thought he had atoned for.

He had pleaded his case in nearly two dozen media interviews. In December, he video-called into a congressional hearing where former Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem promised to look into his case.

Title: Human Strategy In An AI-Accelerated Workflow
Author: hello@smashingmagazine.com (Carrie Webster)
Summary: UX design is evolving as AI rapidly automates tasks like wireframes and prototypes, raising fears of job displacement. However, the core of UX—navigating ambiguity, advocating for users, and solving complex problems—remains irreplaceable. While automation handles artifact creation, human-centered design skills are essential for meaningful, effective solutions.
Content:
UX design is entering a new phase, with designers shifting from makers of outputs to directors of intent. AI can now generate wireframes, prototypes, and even design systems in minutes, but UX has never been only about creating interfaces. It’s about navigating ambiguity, advocating for humans in systems optimized for efficiency, and solving their problems through thoughtful design.

I’ve been working in User Experience design for more than twenty years. Long enough to have held many job titles, from when stakeholders asked us to “just make it pretty” to when wireframes were delivered as annotated PDFs. I’ve seen many tools come and go over the years, methodologies rise and fall, and entire platforms disappear.

Yet, nothing has unsettled designers quite like AI. When generative AI tools first entered my workflow, my reaction wasn’t excitement — it was unease, with a little curiosity. Watching an interface appear in seconds, complete with sensible spacing, readable typography, and halfway-decent copy, triggered a very real fear: if a machine can do this, where does that leave me?

That fear is now widespread. Designers at every level ask the same question, often quietly: “Will an AI agent replace me by next week, month, or year?” While the difference between next week and next year is significant, it depends on where you are in your career and how quickly your employer chooses to adopt AI tools. I have been fortunate in some roles to work with organizations that haven’t allowed the use of AI due to data security concerns. For those interested, discussions about AI in design are ongoing on platforms like Reddit.

Fearing the takeover of AI in our roles is not irrational. We’re AI generating wireframes, prototypes, personas, usability summaries, accessibility suggestions, and entire design systems. Tasks that once took days can now be completed in minutes.

Here’s the uncomfortable truth: if your role is largely about producing artifacts, drawing buttons, aligning components, or translating instructions into screens, then parts of that work are already being automated. Still, UX design has never truly been about just creating a user interface. It’s about navigating ambiguity, advocating for humans within systems optimized for efficiency, and translating complex human needs into effective solutions.

Title: Lebanon at ‘Tipping Point’ as It Seeks to Disarm Hezbollah
Author: Christina Goldbaum
Summary: Lebanon's government has struggled to disarm Hezbollah amid rising tensions with Israel. Recent Israeli airstrikes targeted Hezbollah positions in Beirut, displacing residents. Experts warn this could lead to civil conflict or Hezbollah disarmament, as Lebanon faces escalating violence after Hezbollah's retaliation for Iran's leader's death. The situation remains tense and unpredictable.
Content:
For much of the past year, Lebanon’s government has walked a tightrope in its dealings with the Iranian-backed armed group Hezbollah as it has moved to disarm the militants and curb their influence in Lebanese politics. Now, as Lebanon faces a rapidly escalating conflict between Hezbollah and Israel, the country is awaiting whether the government will seize this moment to take decisive action against Hezbollah—and how the group will respond.

Overnight, the Israeli military bombed Hezbollah positions on the southern edge of Beirut, with explosions audible across the city. The bombardment displaced thousands of residents from the densely populated area, many of whom spent the night on the streets of downtown Beirut. 

“This is the tipping point,” said Sami Nader, director of the Institute of Political Science at Saint Joseph University of Beirut. “Either we have the dark scenario where the army clashes with Hezbollah and there is civil strife, or Hezbollah abides by the government decision and they disarm.”

Lebanese officials have had to strike a delicate balance over the past year: appeasing demands from the United States and other allies to act swiftly against Hezbollah, while proceeding cautiously to avoid clashes between Lebanese soldiers and militants—a scenario many fear could ignite civil conflict. 

However, when Hezbollah began firing at Israel this week in retaliation for the killing of Iran’s Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, and the subsequent escalation of fighting, that balance appeared to unravel.

Title: Trump’s war aim: Iran’s ‘unconditional surrender’
Author: Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
Summary: President Trump called for Iran’s “unconditional surrender” amid ongoing military conflict, suggesting regime change and influence over Iran’s leadership. He emphasized that surrender could mean military defeat or Tehran’s official capitulation. The shift expands U.S. goals from destroying Iran’s missile and nuclear capabilities to regime change, following the killing of Iran’s supreme leader.
Content:
President Donald Trump outlined his administration’s ultimate goal for the war in Iran on Friday: “unconditional surrender” by the Islamic Republic’s leaders.

In a Truth Social post, Trump wrote that “there will be no deal with Iran except UNCONDITIONAL SURRENDER!” Following Tehran’s submission to the ongoing military campaign, he suggested there should be “the selection of a GREAT & ACCEPTABLE Leader(s),” after which the U.S. and its allies would work to “bring Iran back from the brink of destruction, making it economically bigger, better, and stronger than ever before.”

It’s not the first time Trump has called for Iran’s “unconditional surrender.” The president issued a warning to Tehran using those same words during a 12-day war last June — just four days before the U.S. struck multiple of Iran’s nuclear sites.

Later on Friday, Trump said that “surrender” did not necessarily mean a formal announcement from Tehran, but could also be a complete defeat of its military forces. “Unconditional surrender could be that [the Iranians] announce it. But it could also be when they can’t fight any longer because they don’t have anyone or anything to fight with,” he told Axios in an interview.

Trump’s message, coming after days of mixed signals from administration officials, indicates that the U.S. has no intention of providing Tehran with an off-ramp for a deal. It also clarifies the administration’s ultimate aims nearly a week into a war that has already claimed several American lives, as well as those of U.S. allies and Iranian citizens.

The post diverges from what the president said when he announced the U.S. operation against Iran last Saturday. In a video statement at the time, Trump said the goals were to “destroy” Tehran’s missiles and missile industry, “annihilate” its navy, and prevent Iran from developing nuclear weapons.

Now, the president has expanded those aims to include regime change. Trump had already indicated that he wants to influence the selection of Iran’s next leader after the joint U.S.-Israeli operation killed the Islamic Republic’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, over the weekend. He told POLITICO in an interview Thursday that he plans to “have a big impact” in that process.

Speaking to the press in the Oval Office on Tuesday, Trump said he preferred to work with “somebody from within” the current regime — though he admitted that the number of options was dwindling as “most of the people we had in mind are dead.”

Title: 'This is wrong,' Lebanese resident says as hundreds of thousands displaced amid Israeli strikes
Summary: Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon have killed at least 217 people and displaced hundreds of thousands, mainly in Beirut’s southern suburbs. Residents are sleeping outdoors amid ongoing clashes between Israel and Hezbollah, with fears of a humanitarian catastrophe. Israel has ordered Lebanese civilians to evacuate southern Lebanon, intensifying the conflict's impact.
Content:
Israeli airstrikes continued to pound Lebanon on Friday, forcing hundreds of thousands to flee their homes in densely populated neighborhoods to shelter elsewhere, as aid groups warned the escalating conflict could lead to a humanitarian catastrophe. According to Lebanon's Health Ministry, at least 217 people have been killed and hundreds more injured since the conflict intensified on Monday.

Displaced people fleeing Israeli airstrikes in the southern suburbs of Beirut, such as Dahiyeh, are sleeping on the streets, on cars, and on the beach. Jamal Seifeddin, 43, who spent the night outside in downtown Beirut, described the situation: "We’re sleeping here in the streets — some in cars, some on the street, some on the beach. No one even brought a blanket."

The southern suburbs of Beirut were again targeted by Israeli airstrikes as Israel engaged in exchanges of fire with Hezbollah, the Iranian-backed militia. Following U.S. and Israeli attacks against Iran that began a week ago, Hezbollah launched missiles and drones into Israel on Monday for the first time in over a year. Israel responded with bombardments of southern Lebanon and Beirut's southern suburbs.

Residents fleeing the area expressed fears for their safety. Hadi Kaakour, a resident fleeing Beirut's southern suburbs, said he is uncertain he will be safe anywhere, stating, "We don't put anything past [Israel], they will strike us no matter where we go." Others voiced frustration over Lebanon being drawn into the conflict, with Yousef Nabulsi saying, "We got sucked into a mess that we have nothing to do with. People have been displaced and are now staying on the streets, and this is wrong."

On Thursday, Israel's military ordered Lebanese residents out of Beirut's southern suburbs, including areas controlled by Hezbollah, as well as parts of the eastern Bekaa Valley, after directing people to evacuate sections of southern Lebanon.

Title: Justice Department releases Epstein files that include uncorroborated Trump allegation, after complaints
Author: Chris Iorfida
Summary: The U.S. Justice Department released previously undisclosed files related to Jeffrey Epstein, including interviews with a woman alleging she was assaulted by Trump as a teen and claims of Epstein’s schemes. The documents, initially withheld, are part of the Epstein Transparency Act. The White House dismissed the woman's allegations as baseless, and some claims are considered untrue and sensationalist.
Content:
The U.S. Justice Department has released additional files connected to the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, including descriptions of interviews with a woman making allegations against President Donald Trump, a former Epstein associate. The department said Thursday the files had been "incorrectly coded as duplicative," and therefore not originally published. The department has faced scrutiny in Congress over its handling of the documents, which it is required to make public as part of the Epstein Transparency Act, prompted by efforts from both Democratic and Republican legislators.

Some of the new records pertain to a woman who contacted the FBI shortly after Epstein’s 2019 arrest and claimed that a man named “Jeff” living in Hilton Head, South Carolina, had raped her there in the 1980s when she was around 13 years old. The woman told agents she didn’t know the man’s identity at the time, but decades later concluded he was Jeffrey Epstein after a friend texted her his photo from a news story.

The woman was interviewed four times by FBI agents and over time added a host of other claims, including that Epstein had schemed to have her mother sent to prison, beaten her, arranged sexual encounters with other men, and once flew her to either New Jersey or New York in the mid-1980s, where she claimed to have bitten Trump after he tried to sexually assault her. She eventually declined to answer additional questions and broke off contact with agents, according to the files.

White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt called the woman's claims "completely baseless accusations, backed by zero credible evidence." The Justice Department has cautioned that some of the documents include "untrue and sensationalist claims" against Trump. It is not precisely known when the Epstein and Trump relationship began, but the earliest publicly available period is the late 1980s.

Title: Carney approval high in Sask., Liberals at 49 per cent national voter support: poll
Author: Postmedia News
Summary: A poll shows high approval ratings for Prime Minister Mark Carney in Saskatchewan, with 59% support in Manitoba and Saskatchewan. The Liberal government has 59% approval, while support for the Bloc Québécois and NDP remains low. Conservative support has declined to around 37-38%, and most Canadians prefer delaying the next election until 2029.
Content:
High approval ratings in Saskatchewan for Prime Minister Mark Carney show what happens when a federal leader doesn’t go out of their way to pick fights with certain regions of the country, the executive vice-president of a national pollster said. 

The poll indicated that Alberta showed a 53 per cent approval rating for Carney, while Manitoba and Saskatchewan were at 59 per cent. Leger executive vice-president Andrew Enns noted Saskatchewan Premier Scott Moe has taken two international trips with Carney and has come back with results for his constituents.

The poll also revealed that support for the Bloc Québécois fell to five per cent, compared to six per cent in January. Support for the New Democratic Party remained stable at five per cent. Additionally, 59 per cent of respondents expressed satisfaction with the performance of the Liberal government led by Carney.

“We’re actually a full year in of having Prime Minister Carney managing the affairs of the government,” Enns said. “They’ve done a very good job of keeping government activity, government message, on track in terms of what Canadians are expecting of their government during these times.”

Enns stated that the poll is “not good news” for the Conservatives, whose support has remained around 37 to 38 per cent over the past 18 months. 

“Now suddenly they have lost some support,” said Enns. “I think it probably should be concerning to them.” He suggested this could be due to their old messaging falling flat or possibly related to Carney’s personal approval ratings. Some conservative-minded voters seem to favor Carney’s approach.

Speculation about Carney calling an early election to secure a majority has persisted, as the Liberals remain strong in the polls. However, about half of Canadians polled believe the next election should be in 2029, as per the fixed election date legislation.

“Canadians don’t want an election now, they quite frankly don’t want one this spring,” Enns said. “And when you like what you’re getting, which 60 per cent of Canadians do in terms of how the government’s performing and how the prime minister is performing, they’re not interested in changing that.”

Nearly 71 per cent of Liberal voters support an election in 2029. Notably, nearly 24 per cent of Conservative voters would like an election now; 19 per cent would prefer an election this spring, and 20 per cent would like an election at a different time.

Title: Warm to early sounds of spring: Five things to do this weekend in Saskatoon, March 6-8
Author: Jocelyn Bennett, Saskatoon StarPhoenix
Summary: A Women’s Day march was held at Vimy Memorial, featuring a book reading and musical performances. Events included "An Evening with the Bromantics," a brass band concert debuting new works, and a concert by singer-songwriter Eric Kane. The Saskatoon Symphony performed "Spring Fever," and Yurii Kolotylyn presented Ukrainian and English standup comedy.
Content:
Commemorating International Women’s Day, a women’s march was held at the Vimy Memorial in Kiwainis Memorial Park on Sunday at 1 p.m. The event included a book reading, a performance of piano pieces, and some favorite cover songs. Nasser was joined by Bart McKay and Murray Pulver for the performance.

Enjoy a modern take on classic music with "An Evening with the Bromantics," which brings the 1950s to the stage in a tribute to the golden era. The six-piece Saskatchewan band puts its own spin on the sounds of the decade, exploring Motown and early rock with songs from Roy Orbison, The Platters, Buddy Holly, and Sam Cooke. The all-ages concert took place Friday at the Broadway Theatre at 8 p.m.

The Bridge City Brass Band presented traditional and contemporary brass band music. Alongside familiar tunes, the concert featured the Saskatoon premiere of "A Gallimaufry Suite" by Philip Harper and "Eternity" by Michael Bojesen. The concert was held Sunday at Grace-Westminster United Church at 2:30 p.m.

Canadian singer-songwriter Eric Kane stopped in Saskatoon on his "Love is a Weapon pt2" tour. Spreading a message of self-love and growth, Kane’s music translates personal pain into poetic lyricism, navigating grief from the loss of his brother, resilience, and emotional recovery. The concert, limited to ages 19 and older, took place Friday at Art Bar at 7 p.m.

The Saskatoon Symphony Orchestra presented "Spring Fever," with guest conductor Tania Miller leading the orchestra on a musical journey from darkness to light, reflecting the joy of winter transitioning into spring. Principal bassoonist Stephanie Unverricht took center stage for Mozart’s Bassoon Concerto. Other works for the evening included pieces by Beethoven and Schumann.

Yurii Kolotylyn brought his comedy to the stage with a full-length standup show performed in Ukrainian, followed by an English version. The shows, limited to ages 19 and older, were held Saturday at Art Bar at 7 p.m. in Ukrainian and at 9 p.m. in English.

Title: Dillon Brooks, Canadian NBA player with the Phoenix Suns, arrested for suspicion of DUI after traffic stop
Summary: Phoenix Suns forward Dillon Brooks was arrested in Scottsdale for suspicion of DUI early Friday morning after a traffic stop. He was released after booking around 3:30 a.m. Brooks, sidelined since February 21 with a fractured hand, is expected to miss several weeks. The Suns are gathering information; Brooks was having his best season, averaging 20.9 points per game.
Content:
Phoenix Suns forward Dillon Brooks was arrested for suspicion of driving under the influence early Friday morning in Scottsdale, Arizona, police said. Officers conducted a traffic stop in Scottsdale at roughly 1 a.m., and after an investigation, Brooks was taken into custody. He was released following the booking process around 3:30 a.m.

The 30-year-old from Mississauga, Ontario, has not played since February 21 due to a fractured left hand. He is expected to be out until at least late March after undergoing surgery.

The Suns issued a statement saying, "We are aware of the situation involving Dillon Brooks and are gathering more information. We have no further comment at this time."

The team played the Chicago Bulls at home on Thursday night, just hours before his arrest, losing 105-103. Before his injury, Brooks was having the best season of his NBA career, averaging a career-high 20.9 points per game. He joined the Suns as part of the trade that sent Kevin Durant to the Rockets during the offseason.

Title: Pam Bondi’s in trouble with Republicans on Capitol Hill
Author: Hailey Fuchs
Summary: Attorney General Pam Bondi faces a House subpoena over her handling of Jeffrey Epstein files amid Republican scrutiny. While the White House and DOJ support her, some GOP lawmakers are pushing for her testimony. Her political future is uncertain, especially after President Trump’s recent personnel changes and her ongoing controversy.
Content:
Pam Bondi’s in trouble with Republicans on Capitol Hill. The attorney general will soon have to appear before a House committee under subpoena.

Attorney General Pam Bondi was already in trouble with congressional Republicans. Now she could be facing an even more serious threat to her political future after President Donald Trump ousted Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem, showing his willingness to remove Cabinet members who lose trust within the GOP.

Bondi is under intense scrutiny for her handling of the Jeffrey Epstein files. As many as 20 Republicans might be prepared to support efforts to punish her for slowwalking the release of the materials, according to a Democrat leading the charge. Five Republicans joined Democrats on the House Oversight and Government Reform Committee Wednesday to subpoena her testimony.

The White House is signaling confidence in Bondi’s leadership. Abigail Jackson, a White House spokesperson, pointed to Trump’s remarks during an unrelated event where he called Bondi a “terrific person” who is proving “how tough she is,” and expressed belief that she will demonstrate her strength over the next three years.

“Attorney General Pam Bondi has worked tirelessly to successfully implement the President’s law and order agenda,” Jackson said. “The President has full faith in the Attorney General.”

Justice Department spokesperson Natalie Baldassarre praised Bondi for her efforts to deliver transparency in the Epstein case and comply with congressional mandates for the files’ release. She stated that critics “refuse to accept the truth” and accused them of engaging in “laughable antics” to score political points at the expense of victims. Baldassarre also highlighted “DOJ Wins” and included quotes from Congressional Republicans praising Bondi.

Noem’s situation was different. She oversaw an agency whose federal immigration enforcement agents shot and killed two U.S. citizens in Minnesota, faced questions about spending hundreds of millions of taxpayer dollars on a self-promotional ad campaign, and clashed with border czar Tom Homan. However, her recent congressional hearings exposed a lack of confidence among Republicans in her leadership and demonstrated how much Trump is influenced by lawmakers’ sentiments. For Bondi, the situation is becoming increasingly...

Title: Israeli troops push into Lebanon for yet another war with Iran's proxy Hezbollah
Author: Unknown
Summary: Israeli troops have entered Lebanon near the border, securing strategic positions amid increased tensions with Hezbollah, which has launched rockets and drones at northern Israel. Israel is evacuating southern Lebanon and aims to disarm Hezbollah, with plans for potential operations beyond the Litani River. The conflict follows recent air strikes and a large-scale civilian exodus.
Content:
Israeli troops have entered Lebanon for what appears to be another escalation involving Iran's proxy, Hezbollah. Along the northern border, bursts of machine-gun fire from inside Lebanon are loud and persistent as new Israeli forces push forward to secure strategic positions near the border. The sound of air strikes echoes over the remains of Shia villages destroyed during Israel's last ground war there in 2024.

A senior military official stated on Thursday that Israeli ground forces are taking additional hilltops inside Lebanon, describing it as a defensive operation aimed at better protecting northern Israeli communities. However, there has been a significant military build-up, with dozens of tanks and armored bulldozers recently positioned right by the border, fueling speculation of a possible full-scale invasion.

Israel has issued a large-scale evacuation order for southern Lebanon, extending roughly 27 kilometers (16 miles) inside the country. The Israel Defense Forces Chief of Staff, Eyal Zamir, has said the objective in Lebanon is disarming Hezbollah and that efforts will continue until that goal is achieved. A senior military official, speaking anonymously, indicated that forces may operate as far as the Litani River and beyond if instructed, with plans ready for immediate action.

Hezbollah, allied with Iran, joined the conflict on Monday and has been launching rockets and drones at northern Israel daily, most of which are intercepted by Israeli air defenses. Residents in an Israeli border community targeted by a drone on Tuesday expressed reluctance to leave, questioning where they would go, citing increased danger in larger cities like Jerusalem and Tel Aviv.

Israeli opposition leader Yair Lapid suggested that Israel might establish a "sterile zone" in southern Lebanon, similar to the Yellow Line in Gaza, which marks the boundary between Israeli-held territory and that controlled by Hamas. He proposed creating an area with no Lebanese villages, arguing that such measures are a response to Lebanon hosting terrorist organizations.

In the past few days, hundreds of thousands of Lebanese civilians have been fleeing Israeli air strikes, less than 18 months after a ceasefire ended the previous conflict.

Title: Complex repairs needed after semi collision with Saskatoon overpass
Author: thestarphoenix
Summary: A semi hauling heavy equipment struck the Highway 11, Highway 16, and Circle Drive overpass in Saskatoon, causing significant damage. Traffic was reduced to one lane, with northbound and eastbound closures. Repairs are underway, but the timeline is unclear. No injuries were reported, and authorities are investigating the incident.
Content:
Traffic moved through a single open lane beneath the Circle Drive overpass on Highway 11 as crews assessed damage after a semi hauling heavy equipment struck the structure Thursday afternoon. Saskatoon Police said the collision caused significant damage to the overpass at the Highway 11, Highway 16, and Circle Drive cloverleaf.

Repairs are needed on the busy overpass in Saskatoon after cargo on a semi-trailer collided with the concrete structure. The City of Saskatoon stated that the extent of the damage or the cost of repairs to the section of the cloverleaf at Circle Drive and Highway 11 was not immediately known after the collision Thursday afternoon.

Planning for repairs was underway on Friday, though a timeline was not available due to “the complexity and extent of the required work,” according to a city news release. Saskatoon police reported that a semi with a trailer carrying equipment on Thursday around 5 p.m., traveling northbound on Highway 11 from Regina and entering the city limits at the Circle Drive overpass, was too high for the structure and caused significant damage.

Northbound traffic on Highway 11 and eastbound traffic on Circle Drive were immediately closed off for hours before one lane in each direction was reopened. No injuries were reported. City engineers, Saskatoon police, emergency services, and collision analysts were all on the scene. Analysts worked with Saskatchewan Highway Patrol to investigate the incident.

Work began on Friday morning to safely remove loose concrete and address safety concerns. Southbound traffic on Highway 11 and westbound traffic on Highway 16 remained unaffected. Northbound semi trucks on Highway 11 were being detoured around the area at Township 360 via Range Road 3050, according to the city.

Title: Despite off-night shooting, Huskies advance to women's hoops semifinal
Author: thestarphoenix
Summary: The University of Saskatchewan Huskies advanced to the U Sports women’s basketball semifinals with a 71-55 win over UBC, despite shooting poorly. Led by Gage Grassick’s 25 points, they aim for a second consecutive national title. They will face Laval, which upset Toronto Metropolitan in the quarterfinal, on Saturday at 4 p.m.
Content:
Though the University of Saskatchewan Huskies were off-target on the offensive end, they were sharp enough defensively to make up for it as they began their pursuit for back-to-back national basketball titles.

In their quarterfinal on Thursday versus Canada West rival British Columbia at the U Sports Women’s Final 8 in Quebec City, the fifth-placed Huskies shot just 24 per cent from three-point range and 48 per cent from the free throw line, but overcame those struggles by locking down the fourth-placed Thunderbirds. Holding UBC to 34 per cent shooting from the field and never allowing more than 15 points in any quarter, the Huskies advanced to Saturday’s semifinal with a 71-55 victory.

Winners of U Sports crowns in 2016, 2020 and 2025, the Huskies have another national title within reach. The team, undefeated in the regular season, lost in the Canada West semifinal but received the lone wild card to nationals.

“I think if we can defend like that and we can have a bit better shooting night, then we’ll give ourselves a chance no matter who we’re playing against,” Huskies head coach Lisa Thomaidis said following the game.

First-team all-Canadian Gage Grassick led the Huskies with a game-high 25 points, while adding three steals and four rebounds. Ella Murphy Wiebe scored 16 points and hauled down eight rebounds. Maya Flindell and Andrea Dodig each scored 11 points for the team. Sara Toneguzzi had a team-high 21 points for UBC.

In Saturday’s semifinal, the Huskies take on No. 8- and host Laval. In a first-round shocker, the Rouge et Or knocked off top-ranked Toronto Metropolitan 57-54. Sabrine Khelifi, who led her team with 14 points, banked a three-pointer from the top of the arc with 15 seconds left in the fourth quarter to break the tie and propel her squad to the medal round.

The Huskies tip off Saturday against Laval at 4 p.m. On the other side of the bracket, Canada West champion and third-ranked Calgary Dinos advanced with a 58-45 win over sixth-ranked McGill. In Saturday’s other semifinal, Calgary faces off against No. 2- and host New Brunswick, which handled seventh-ranked Carleton Ravens 80-63.

Title: T.I. Unfazed by 50 Cent's Diss and Threat to Drop Scandalous Doc
Author: C. Vernon Coleman II
Summary: T.I. dismisses 50 Cent's threats to release a scandalous documentary about him and Tiny, responding with diss tracks and social media posts. T.I. challenges 50 Cent to a lyrical or documentary battle, criticizing his motives and calling him out on various issues, including family and credibility. The feud continues with both artists exchanging insults.
Content:
T.I. is unfazed by 50 Cent's apparent diss and threat to put out a scandalous documentary about Tip and Tiny. The ongoing beef between T.I. and 50 Cent continues, with 50 sharing a since-deleted post threatening to release a "Surviving T.I. and Tiny" documentary and appearing to throw shade at T.I. and the Harris family on the new Power Origins theme song.

T.I. responded to 50's threat and the shade on the new song. He captioned a video of himself performing his single "Let Em Know" in the streets, saying, "We can go bar for bar, hit for hit, or doc for doc... either way all you gon do is show how much of a FN u is. Let’s play. Nobody fears you over here Boo Boo…"

In a follow-up post, T.I. shared a new song called "Trauma Bond," in which he raps about his family and throws shade at 50. He said, "These ni**as, the suckas for clicks, bi**hes for algorithms/Your mama ran the streets she shoulda left the daddy with him," and added, "@50cent Drop whatever you choose, just please keep showing da world You a H*!!! #OnClay."

He continued, "[And] You still ain’t tell us why you in 4K 'D**k Pulling' wit the smile of a Sissy???? Pandora’s box is ajar sir… Good luck FN You suck. P.S. Me & mine till the end of time Issa 'Trauma Bond'!!!!"

Title: Flight paths squeezed as Iran conflict closes more airspace
Author: Unknown
Summary: The closure of southern Azerbaijan's airspace due to conflict disrupts global flights, forcing airlines to reroute north or south, increasing travel times and fuel costs. Western airlines avoid Russian, Iranian, and Iraqi airspace, causing congestion in northern Azerbaijan. Gulf airlines shift routes through Egypt, Saudi Arabia, and Oman, with limited capacity from Qatar. Overall, air traffic is significantly reduced and more complex.
Content:
The closure of airspace over the southern half of Azerbaijan is putting additional pressure on airlines scrambling to respond to disruption caused by the conflict in the Gulf. Flight traffic had already been severely disrupted in a region which in normal times acts as a significant hub for the global airline industry. The Buta drone attack on the small Central Asian country has squeezed many flights into a narrow corridor across the north of the country. 

Aviation expert John Strickland said there was now a "very tight range of options for airlines" trying to navigate the current situation. Western airlines have had to avoid Russian airspace, including Siberia, since the invasion of Ukraine in 2022. Now all flights are also steering clear of Iran and Iraq airspace, opting for alternative routes to the north or south. Flightradar24 data maps show real-time congestion in an approximately 100 km wide strip across the north of Azerbaijan, a country roughly the size of Portugal. Late on Friday, Flightradar24 data showed planes using northern Azerbaijan's airspace but not flying over the south of the country.

Gulf airlines have begun to operate more flights out of the UAE and Oman, helping to evacuate stranded travelers, but there are no flights out of Qatar and capacity remains well below normal levels, according to Opsgroup, which provides risk information for the industry. "The central corridor across Iran, Iraq, and the Gulf is effectively closed, so most traffic is going either north via the Caucasus and Afghanistan, or south via Egypt, Saudi Arabia, and Oman," said David Mumford, an international operations specialist at Opsgroup. "Both routes are longer and busier than usual, so flight times and fuel burn are higher."

Air traffic sharply reduced after the attacks began on Saturday as airlines were forced to use more limited corridors. Aviation consultant Strickland noted that passengers could find their flight times extended due to longer routings. "Flights already doing circuitous routing have even less choice," he said. Some airlines had begun to adapt routes in recent weeks as tension built in the Gulf, and this week even more flights were forced to switch to routes further south to avoid the conflict zone.

There was likely to be congestion at busy times of the day, such as west to east in the afternoon and evening when Europe to Asia flights are typically scheduled, and east to west early in the morning when flights tend to come from Asia to Europe. This represented "another big complication for airlines' planning" on top of usual factors like meteorological conditions and the impact of delays on aircraft and crew scheduling. Most airlines are not currently adding stops to accommodate the longer routes, but Qantas has added a refueling stop in Singapore on its non-stop flight from Perth to London.

Title: OSHA probing fatality at Rivian warehouse
Author: Sean O'Kane
Summary: OSHA is investigating the death of 61-year-old Kevin Lancaster at Rivian's Illinois warehouse, who died after being pinned between a tractor trailer and a loading dock. The incident occurred near Rivian’s factory, and the investigation may take six months. Rivian expressed condolences and is cooperating with authorities amid prior safety scrutiny at the facility.
Content:
The Occupational Safety and Health Administration has opened an investigation into the death of a worker at a Rivian warehouse in Illinois. The agency said the probe could take up to six months.

The man, identified by local authorities as 61-year-old Kevin Lancaster, reportedly died from “blunt traumatic compressional injuries” after getting pinned between a tractor trailer and a loading dock at the facility, which is located just a few miles from Rivian’s factory. Lancaster was reportedly trapped in that spot for around 20 minutes before firefighters were able to reach him. Emergency crews responded to a call at 1:40 p.m. local time, and Lancaster was pronounced dead at a local medical center at 2:33 p.m. The Normal Police Department and the McLean County Coroner are still investigating Lancaster’s death.

Rivian issued a statement saying, “Safety at our facilities is our top priority. Unfortunately yesterday afternoon, a contractor passed away after an incident at our warehouse. Our sympathy and thoughts are with their family and friends. We are working with the Normal Police Department on its investigation.”

The safety of Rivian’s factory in Normal, Illinois, became a subject of scrutiny in 2024 after Bloomberg News reported 16 “serious” violations levied on the company across that year and the previous one. Despite this, Rivian has received only one violation at the Illinois manufacturing plant since that report was published. OSHA told Bloomberg that Rivian “has improved their safety and health team and are very cooperative with the OSHA process.”

Rivian assembles its R1 pickup truck, R1 SUV, and EDV commercial electric van at its 4.3-million-square-foot factory in Normal. The company is expanding the facility by another 1.1 million square feet to accommodate its next EV, the R2. Once complete, the factory will have the capacity to assemble 215,000 vehicles.

Title: King, Domani Strike Back at 50 Cent with Two Angry Diss Tracks
Author: Trent Fitzgerald
Summary: King and Domani released diss tracks on March 6 targeting 50 Cent for dissing T.I. and the Harris family on the Power Origins theme song. King’s "Droptop" criticizes 50 Cent’s behavior, while Domani’s "PU$Y" challenges his toughness. 50 Cent responded with provocative lyrics and a photoshopped image implying disrespect toward T.I. and Tiny, escalating the feud.
Content:
King and Domani dropped two angry diss tracks after 50 Cent appeared to have dissed T.I. and the Harris family on the new Power Origins theme song. On Friday (March 6), King and Domani released the songs on their Instagram pages in retaliation against 50 Cent for disrespecting T.I. and the Harris family.

King's diss track is called "Droptop." In the song, he criticizes 50 Cent for talking too much smack and alleges that he has a history of beating women. "You were made from 50 Cent because ya mama gave it cheap," King raps.

Meanwhile, Domani delivered his diss track titled "PU$Y." On the haunting song, Domani challenges 50 Cent's tough-guy image. "I smell p***y / How about you come here and try to push me / That suga water got ya, now you turn sweet, huh / I smell p***y / Ni**a, I read you like a book read / Come bring ya a*s to school quit playin' hookie / I smell p***y," he raps.

Both songs respond to 50 Cent, who featured the Harris family on the theme song for his upcoming television show, Power Origins. The track, featuring R&B singer Leon Thomas, includes street rap about the drug trade. 50 Cent raps, "I'm back on my dope boy grammar / Your daddy made your mama eat every box in Atlanta," and mentions discreet activities with, "Freak sh*t / Peep sh*t / Keep sh*t, on the low / But everybody know." He captioned his Instagram post about the song, "50cent Ft Leon Thomas (No one told us what we’re here for) POWER ORIGINS theme song!"

50 Cent further escalated tensions by posting a photoshopped image of T.I. with Tiny eating from a cut-up cardboard box, possibly implying that 50's lyric, "Your daddy made your mama eat every box in Atlanta," was directed at T.I. and the Harris family matriarch. He captioned the post, "LOL...They are gonna learn to leave me alone."

The feud between 50 Cent and the Harris family is intensifying.

Title: Life EV officially owns Rad Power Bikes now
Author: Kirsten Korosec
Summary: Life Electric Vehicles (Life EV) acquired Rad Power Bikes for $13.2 million, including its assets and IP, after Rad Power filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in December. Life EV plans to maintain Rad Power’s operations, expand retail presence, and support existing customers. Based in Florida, Life EV focuses on acquiring and scaling e-bike brands, with Rad Power being its most notable acquisition.
Content:
Life Electric Vehicles Holdings, also known as Life EV, officially owns the intellectual property, inventory, and certain operating assets of Rad Power Bikes. Life EV acquired Rad Power for $13.2 million. Rad Power Bikes, a prominent electric bike company that raised nearly $330 million in venture capital, filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection in December. The company had struggled for months prior to its bankruptcy filing and had warned employees that it might have to shut down without new capital.

Life EV intends to keep the company intact and said it will continue retail operations under the Rad Power Bikes brand in the United States. It also plans to expand its retail footprint in select key markets. The company pledged to support existing customers regarding warranties and gift cards.

Based in Florida, Life EV has built its business by acquiring, developing, and scaling electric bicycle and micro-mobility brands. While Rad Power is its most high-profile acquisition, the company also holds an equity interest in LEV Manufacturing, Inc., which acquired the Serial 1 premium electric bicycle brand originally developed and spun off from Harley-Davidson.

In a statement, Life EV said the acquisition aligns with its broader strategy of expansion across North America.

Title: Weekly Chartstopper: March 6, 2026
Author: Michael Normyle
Summary: Since the Iran conflict began, energy prices have surged—up to 35% in US oil and 65% in European LNG—raising inflation risks. Markets expect fewer Fed rate cuts, with the 10-year Treasury yield rising to 4.15%. Despite a job loss report, the Nasdaq-100 declined only 1%, amid ongoing economic uncertainty. Key upcoming data includes CPI, PCE, GDP, and job reports.
Content:
US Markets Weekly Chartstopper: March 6, 2026

March 06, 2026 — 05:00 pm EST

Since the conflict with Iran began on Saturday, markets have been trying to assess its economic impact, primarily through higher energy prices. The conflict has effectively halted traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, which transports 20-25% of global oil and liquefied natural gas (LNG), and has shuttered energy facilities in the region.

This week, international and U.S. oil prices have risen 30% and 35%, respectively, reaching their highest levels since 2023. European LNG prices are up 65%, while U.S. LNG prices have increased by 10%. JPMorgan estimates that if oil prices remain at these levels, U.S. headline inflation could increase by 0.3 percentage points, and U.S. GDP growth could decrease by 0.6 percentage points. 

Given this likely boost to inflation, markets have reduced their expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts this year by 20 basis points to around 40 basis points. This adjustment follows a significant jobs report miss, with the economy losing 92,000 jobs in February—against expectations of a 55,000 gain—and broad-based losses across sectors. However, it’s important not to overinterpret a single month’s data, as the private sector has averaged about 20,000 jobs gained per month over the past three months.

Despite these developments, the Nasdaq-100® is down just 1% this week, though rising inflation expectations have pushed the 10-year Treasury yield up nearly 20 basis points to 4.15%.

Next Week

Here are the top events to watch:

- Tuesday: NFIB Small Business Optimism (February)
- Wednesday: CPI Inflation (February)
- Thursday: Jobless Claims
- Friday: PCE Inflation and Spending (January), Real GDP (Q4 revision), JOLTS Job Openings (January)

Michael Normyle  
U.S. Economist and Senior Director at Nasdaq

Title: Best Xbox Game Pass games to play this weekend (March 6-8 2026)
Author: Austin Manchester
Summary: March's new game releases, including Pokémon Pokopia and Slay the Spire 2, are not on Xbox Game Pass. Instead, the service offers popular titles like Resident Evil 2, the indie puzzle-platformer Planet of Lana 2, and the newly launched Slay the Spire 2, along with its predecessor, providing a variety of engaging options for subscribers.
Content:
While March is off to a strong start for new games, with Pokémon Pokopia, Slay the Spire 2, and Marathonall releasing this past week, none of those are on Xbox Game Pass. Neither is February's Resident Evil Requiem, one of the year's best games so far and a potential contender for Game of the Year. 

However, there are many excellent games available on Game Pass worth checking out. This week's recommendations include a precursor to Resident Evil Requiem, an indie hit, and a must-play roguelike deckbuilder.

Resident Evil 2 is currently a popular choice, especially since Resident Evil Requiem has become the fastest-selling game in the series’s history. The remake follows rookie cop Leon as he tries to escape Raccoon City police headquarters and uncover the secrets of the Umbrella Corporation. It offers a thrilling, well-paced adventure that can be completed over a weekend. Players can also experience Claire Redfield’s route for additional content.

Planet of Lana 2: Children of the Leaf is a stylish and cinematic indie puzzle-platformer. It tells a deeply personal story through expressive animation, sweeping music, and standout vocal performances, despite using a language that sounds like gibberish. The game introduces new puzzle mechanics, such as using Mui's ability to hypnotize creatures to progress. The sequel opens with a recap of the first game, which is also available on Game Pass, so players can enjoy both titles without prior experience.

Slay the Spire 2 recently launched and is one of the biggest game releases of the year. For those curious about the series, the original Slay the Spire remains available. Released in 2019, it popularized the roguelike deckbuilding genre with its mix of strategic card play, unique visuals, and high replayability. Slay the Spire 2 builds upon these fundamentals, offering even more depth and variety.

Title: U.S. says it worked with Ecuador to bomb alleged drug-trafficking operations site
Author: Unknown
Summary: The U.S. and Ecuador conducted a joint operation targeting a Colombian FARC dissident training camp near Ecuador's border, involving helicopters, aircraft, boats, and drones. The operation aimed to dismantle drug trafficking networks, with no details on casualties. Ecuador's President Noboa emphasizes a crackdown on organized crime and values U.S. support.
Content:
The U.S. and Ecuador carried out a joint operation targeting drug trafficking in Ecuador, authorities in both countries announced on Friday. The U.S. described the operation as "lethal kinetic operations." Neither U.S. Southern Command nor Ecuador's defense ministry provided details on whether anyone was killed or captured during the strike.

The operation involved helicopters, aircraft, river boats, and drones to locate and bomb a drug traffickers' training camp in northeastern Ecuador near the Colombian border, according to Ecuador's defense ministry. The camp belonged to the Comandos de la Frontera (CDF), a Colombian criminal organization composed of FARC dissidents, with a capacity for 50 people.

Ecuador's President Daniel Noboa has prioritized a military crackdown on organized crime. His government has also imposed tariffs on Colombia, accusing it of insufficient efforts to combat drug trafficking. Noboa is scheduled to travel to Miami this weekend to participate in the Trump administration's "Shield of the Americas" summit, which gathers regional leaders to discuss security and organized crime.

The Ecuadorian defense ministry stated, "The United States is a key ally in this fight," and noted that the operation was conducted at Ecuador's request to dismantle narco-terrorist networks. This followed a similar U.S.-Ecuador operation announced earlier this week by U.S. Southern Command.

Title: Resident Evil Requiem is a timely critique of capitalist greed
Author: Josh Broadwell
Summary: Resident Evil Requiem offers a critique of capitalism and selfishness, focusing on Victor Gideon's plan to transfer consciousness into elites using blood experiments at the Raccoon City orphanage. The story highlights themes of exploitation, manipulation, and the dehumanization of vulnerable victims, likening the process to social media manipulation and emphasizing the series' ongoing critique of corporate evil.
Content:
As much as Resident Evil changed over the years, a few things remained constant with the series' attitude toward society. Corporations are bad, rich people are even worse, and corporate scientists are also bad. The themes usually don't go much further than that. But Resident Evil Requiem has a more carefully thought-out critique of capitalism and selfishness more broadly, and it's a timely one as well. 

Victor Gideon, one of Requiem's primary antagonists, is the focal point of Capcom's critique. He wants to realize Oswell Spencer's presumed goal of preserving the consciousness of a "superior" person by transplanting it into another host to make new generations of "elites," not unlike other real-life tech and business moguls. It is revealed that the Raccoon City Orphanage was a front for these experiments, with dozens of clone children created for this purpose. Central to Gideon’s plans is the idea that people's memories and essences are stored in blood, hence all the blood-related work he's doing in the Rhodes Care Home. This concept isn't just Resident Evil hokum; the idea that blood contains part of the human spirit and could transfer it between hosts persisted for centuries, dating back to the Middle Ages.

The ideal result is a person who remains an individual but is inclined to think like Spencer, know what he knows, and behave like him if Gideon’s plans succeed. The operation resembles a gruesome algorithm that takes pieces from everyone's experiences, then adds a predetermined bias to create a result that benefits the creator more than anyone else. This process can be likened to social media, where a handful of malicious individuals try to manipulate what people think and believe.

The connections become even more explicit in the care center’s basement, where Gideon and his team put people through a mulcher to extract their blood for these experiments. If the blood theory is correct, they are not just taking lives—they are using the essence of existence—their memories, souls, and everything that makes them alive—for their own ends, like a plaything. The victims are often vulnerable people who believe they are receiving help, only to be turned into monsters when someone decides it’s time.

Requiem's storytelling takes a long break once Leon Kennedy reaches Raccoon City, but the critique of exploitation and the grotesque abuse of power remains a central theme throughout the narrative.

Title: How to get bricks in Pokémon Pokopia
Author: Josh Broadwell
Summary: In Pokémon Pokopia, players need squishy clay and Burn-type Pokémon (Charmander, Torchic, Litwick, Growlithe, Vulpix) to craft bricks for advanced building projects. Clay is obtained by breaking clay blocks in caves or from Paldean Woopers. Bricks are produced by Pokémon using clay, with no daily limit. Guides and walkthroughs assist with resource collection and game progression.
Content:
This article is part of a directory: Pokémon Pokopia guides and walkthroughs.

You'll need bricks in Pokémon Pokopia to complete more advanced, non-leaf building projects. However, the game doesn't provide explicit instructions early on for how to obtain bricks. You need a specific resource and the help of a certain type of Pokémon, but once you understand the process, making bricks can be easily incorporated into your daily routine.

To get bricks in Pokémon Pokopia, you'll need squishy clay and the assistance of a Pokémon with the Burn specialty. These Pokémon include Charmander, Torchic, Litwick, Growlithe, and Vulpix. Since Charmander is available in the first area and Torchic in Bleak Beach, you'll be able to start making bricks soon after obtaining clay.

With clay in hand, speak to your Burn Pokémon of choice, select "Look at this!", show them the clay, and ask them to turn it into bricks. One lump of squishy clay yields two bricks, and you can process up to 10 lumps at a time. It takes a few minutes for the Pokémon to fire the clay into bricks, but there is no limit on how many times per day you can have them make bricks.

Bricks are not typically found in the wild like rocks. To get squishy clay, you can start harvesting after completing the "Yawn up a Storm" request, which involves rain softening clay trapping Onix. Use Rock Smash to break the clay blocks and collect the squishy clay left behind. There are several dozen clay blocks in the cave system here, and additional clay can be found in the Bleak Beach region.

Additionally, if you create a Pokémon habitat for Paldean Wooper, you may find clumps of squishy clay around that habitat. Wooper has the Litter specialty, causing them to drop specific items at intervals, including squishy clay.

For new players, guides are available to help you upgrade your island, including a Pokédex and Habitat Dex to identify spawn locations and building requirements for summoning your favorite Pokémon. If you're feeling overwhelmed, there are early tips and tricks to help streamline your progress.

There are also walkthroughs for other key requests that advance the story, such as "Brighten Things Up!", "Time to Party!", and "Build the Huge Building!". Multiplayer options are available, but there are restrictions for regular Pokopia multiplayer and the Game Share version.

Title: Nintendo sues the US government for a refund on tariffs
Author: Amanda Silberling
Summary: Nintendo filed a lawsuit in the U.S. Court of International Trade seeking refunds for tariffs imposed under President Trump’s executive orders, which the Supreme Court ruled exceeded presidential authority. The tariffs, collected under the IEEPA, totaled over $200 billion. Nintendo is among more than a thousand companies suing for refunds following the Supreme Court decision.
Content:
Nintendo filed a lawsuit against the U.S. government on Friday over its collection of tariffs from global businesses. The gaming company is seeking a refund for any duties it paid due to President Donald Trump’s executive orders that invoked the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA).

The lawsuit was filed in the U.S. Court of International Trade following a Supreme Court decision that struck down the tariffs imposed under IEEPA, ruling that the president had exceeded his authority. More than a thousand other companies have also sued for refunds on these tariffs, which have resulted in the collection of over $200 billion in total import duties, according to Nintendo’s complaint.

Nintendo confirmed the filing in a statement to Tech Crunch, saying, “We have nothing else to share on the topic.” In response to the Supreme Court’s decision— which President Trump called “extraordinarily anti-American”— tariffs were raised from 10% to 15%. Currently, 24 states have sued, arguing that the president has once again overstepped his powers by making this change.

Title: X is testing a new ad format that connects posts with products
Author: Sarah Perez
Summary: X is testing a new ad format that inserts product recommendations beneath posts referencing the company or its products, initially spotted with Starlink in Europe. The feature, confirmed by X’s head of product, aims to integrate ads seamlessly without traditional ad labels. This follows new “Paid Partnership” labels and broader efforts to attract creators and advertisers, enhancing monetization and competitiveness.
Content:
X is testing a new ad format that inserts a recommendation directly underneath a post that references the company or its products. The initial test, spotted by an X user in Europe, displayed a suggestion to “Get Starlink” beneath a post from a user that said Starlink’s satellite service works great in Portugal. The link, when clicked, directed users to Starlink’s website. X head of product Nikita Bier confirmed the test, responding, “Trying to make an ad product that isn’t an ad.” 

The Starlink ad is not visible to all users at this time, but the placeholder where the ad sits is. If a user views @levelsio’s post from March 6, they will see an outlined box beneath the text. This box currently showcases a random X post unless the user is in a location where the ad test is live. 

In places where the ad displays, several commenters noticed the new addition, with one asking, “lmao, did you add this Starlink button?” In response to a suggestion that X should allow affiliate links in this ad slot, Bier said, “No, then people will lie. I want to trust recommendations on here.” 

The test follows earlier news that the company is rolling out “Paid Partnership” labels for creators. These labels can be applied to posts to comply with social media advertising regulations, instead of requiring creators to use hashtags like “ad” or “paid partnership.” If sponsored posts were combined with embedded links like the one being tested, X could potentially attract more marketers to the platform. This could encourage creators to use the app more, helping it compete against larger social networks such as Instagram, YouTube, and TikTok. 

X has been pursuing creator content for some time—long before it was called “X” and before Elon Musk’s ownership. The platform has introduced various creator products, including payouts for viral content, ad-revenue sharing, and creator subscriptions. Recently, the company revamped its Creator Subscriptions offering with new features, including the ability to monetize individual threads. 

Additionally, X announced that its integrated chatbot Grok can now read X’s long-form content, known as Articles. However, this feature remains underutilized, as many creators prefer publishing lengthy text on their own websites or newsletters.

Title: CyHi Addresses G.O.O.D. Music Breakup on "The G.O.O.D. Ole Days"
Author: Trent Fitzgerald
Summary: Cy Hi addresses Travis Scott, Kid Cudi, and Kanye West in his new song "The G.O.O.D. Ole Days," revealing that his fallout with Travis stemmed from unreciprocated co-writing efforts, while his issues with Kid Cudi relate to siding with Ye during a dispute. He also discusses Ye's leadership challenges and announces a forthcoming album, "The Story of Mr. EGOT."
Content:
Cy Hi addresses Travis Scott, Kid Cudi, and the reasons behind the G.O.O.D. Music breakup in his new song, "The G.O.O.D. Ole Days." On Friday (March 6), Cy Hi released the track, produced by Mark Byrd and Phil Blackman, where he discusses artists from Ye's G.O.O.D. Music label and why the breakup happened.

In the song, the Georgia rapper explains his fallout with G.O.O.D. Music members Travis Scott, Kid Cudi, and others. Cy Hi claims that his friendship with Travis deteriorated when he couldn't get a verse from him in exchange for co-writing Travis's biggest hit, "Sicko Mode." He raps, "I like what he did in fashion, but ni**a just look hella tacky. When I helped him with his album, it went like [11-times] platinum, but I still ain't got my swap and he mad that I'm even askin'."

Regarding Kid Cudi, Cy Hi believes their relationship soured because he sided with Ye during a past disagreement with Cudi. He states, "It could have got real ugly. They said that Ye almost f**k around and killed buddy, and that's why their relationship is still muddy. And I'm caught between it all. Man, ni**a should feel for me."

As for Ye, Cy Hi acknowledges that he was also a reason for G.O.O.D. Music's dissolution but empathizes with the Chicago rapper-producer, noting Ye's role as a leader trying to keep the group together. He lyrically explains, "Okay, I understand that. But you know sometimes Kanye could be a little difficult. I get it, but I understand him in a different way. Y'all just know the beautiful dark twisty Ye. He's just an honest person, but it gets conveyed as somebody with a broken heart against the 808. I ain't going to lie. Sometimes it gets in the business way. He just ain't afraid to say what you didn't say."

Near the end of the song, Cy Hi announces he's dropping a new album titled "The Story of Mr. EGOT" "real soon."

Title: Buy the Spike in CrowdStrike or Okta Stock After Posting Record Profitability?
Author: Zacks
Summary: CrowdStrike and Okta posted strong Q4 results, surpassing expectations with record profitability and revenue growth. CrowdStrike's AI-driven Falcon platform drove 24% sales growth to $1.3 billion, while Okta's subscription and enterprise adoption increased, with sales up 11% to $761 million. Both stocks have surged over 10% since earnings reports.
Content:
Technology

Buy the Spike in CrowdStrike or Okta Stock After Posting Record Profitability?

In a week marked by broader market volatility, cybersecurity firms CrowdStrike (CRWD) and Okta (OKTA) have emerged as outperformers after exceeding their Q4 expectations. Both stocks have surged more than 10% since reporting their Q4 results on Tuesday and Wednesday, respectively.

Both companies delivered standout Q4 reports, showing record profitability, strong revenue growth, expanding recurring revenue bases, and rising demand for cybersecurity and identity solutions.

CrowdStrike’s AI-Driven Security Demand

CrowdStrike’s strong Q1 results were defined by faster-than-expected growth amid surging demand for its Falcon cybersecurity platform as AI-driven cyber threats become more sophisticated. Notably, Falcon is a cloud-native cybersecurity solution that provides advanced endpoint detection and response (EDR), managed detection and response (MDR), and next-generation antivirus capabilities to protect organizations from cyber threats.

Falcon’s growth led to CrowdStrike posting Q4 sales of $1.3 billion, up 24% from a year ago and exceeding estimates of $1.29 billion. Its net new annual recurring revenue (ARR) grew 47% year over year, reaching a quarterly record of $331 million. Importantly, CrowdStrike saw record profitability since going public in 2019, with Q4 EPS rising 9% to $1.12, surpassing expectations of $1.10.

Okta’s Enterprise Adoption & Platform Consolidation

Okta’s standout Q4 report was highlighted by steady subscription growth, expanding enterprise adoption, and strong cash flow, signaling a successful transition for its now unified identity security platform. The consolidated platform allows customers to integrate with nearly any application, service, or cloud through a secure, reliable, and scalable system. Accelerating adoption was attributed to trust from large organizations as they modernize their security architecture.

With solid revenue growth, Okta’s Q4 sales increased 11% to $761 million, beating estimates of $749.1 million. Its remaining performance obligations (RPO) rose 15% year over year, with current RPO growing 12%, indicating strong future revenue potential. Okta also posted record profitability, with Q4 EPS rising 15% to $0.90, beating expectations of $0.85. Full-year results showed annual sales surging 22% to $4.81 billion, with full-year EPS increasing significantly.

Title: Par Petroleum (PARR) Q4 Earnings: How Key Metrics Compare to Wall Street Estimates
Author: Zacks
Summary: Par Petroleum reported Q4 revenue of $1.81 billion, up 5.9% from estimates, but EPS of $1.17 missed the $1.21 forecast. Refining throughput exceeded estimates in several refineries, while gross margins and production costs varied. Overall, revenue was slightly below last year, with mixed performance across segments.
Content:
Technology Par Petroleum (PARR) Q4 Earnings: How Key Metrics Compare to Wall Street Estimates

For the quarter ended December 2025, Par Petroleum (PARR) reported revenue of $1.81 billion, down 1% over the same period last year. EPS came in at $1.17, compared to -$0.79 in the year-ago quarter. The reported revenue represents a surprise of +5.9% over the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.71 billion. With the consensus EPS estimate being $1.21, the EPS surprise was -2.91%.

While investors closely watch year-over-year changes in headline numbers—revenue and earnings—and how they compare to Wall Street expectations to determine their next course of action, some key metrics always provide a better insight into a company's underlying performance. Since these metrics play a crucial role in driving the top- and bottom-line numbers, comparing them with the year-ago numbers and what analysts estimated about them helps investors better project a stock's price performance.

Here is how Par Petroleum performed in the just reported quarter in terms of the metrics most widely monitored and projected by Wall Street analysts:

- Total Refining - Feedstocks Throughput: 190.9 million barrels of oil versus the three-analyst average estimate of 189.1 million barrels.
- Hawaii Refinery - Feedstocks Throughput: 87.10 million barrels per day compared to the 85.96 million barrels per day average estimate.
- Washington Refinery - Feedstocks Throughput: 37.00 million barrels per day versus 36.08 million barrels per day estimated by three analysts.
- Wyoming Refinery - Feedstocks Throughput: 14.40 million barrels per day compared to the 15.53 million barrels per day average estimate.
- Montana Refinery - Feedstocks Throughput: 52.40 million barrels per day compared to the 51.55 million barrels per day estimate.

- Adjusted Gross Margin per barrel - Washington Refinery: $8.32 versus $6.39 estimated by two analysts on average.
- Production costs per barrel - Wyoming Refinery: $13.27 versus the two-analyst average estimate of $10.25.
- Production costs per barrel - Washington Refinery: $4.57 compared to the $4.08 estimate.
- Production costs per barrel - Hawaii Refinery: $4.15 versus the two-analyst average estimate of $4.70.

- Revenues from Refining: $1.75 billion versus the two-analyst estimate of $1.57 billion, representing a year-over-year change of -1.2%.
- Revenues from Retail: $142.28 million compared to the $145.87 million estimate, a +0.5% change year over year.
- Revenues from Logistics: $73.71 million versus the $74 million estimate.

Title: Pokémon Go 'Pokémon 30th Anniversary: All Out' event guide
Author: Julia Lee
Summary: Pokémon Go's 30th Anniversary event runs from March 3-9, celebrating the Kanto region with increased wild spawns, special encounters, and milestone rewards. The "All Out" event from March 7-9 offers legendary raids, regional Ultra Beasts, and exclusive Pokémon, with increased catch and XP bonuses. Baby Pokémon hatch from 2 km eggs, while many Pokémon remain unavailable.
Content:
Pokémon Go's Pokémon 30th Anniversary event is here, celebrating the Kanto region once again. The event runs from March 3-9, with an upgraded "All Out" version taking place from March 7-9, changing spawns and bonuses for the weekend.

During this event, all Kanto Pokémon, including Articuno, Zapdos, and Moltres, can be found in the wild, except for Mewtwo and Mew. A special Go Pass is available, rewarding encounters with various Kanto Pokémon and a free Mewtwo encounter at rank 80. Milestone rewards include doubled catch candy at rank 25 and doubled catch XP at rank 50, with these bonuses tripled if the premium pass is purchased.

The event also features changes to Ditto disguises, though details are still being confirmed.

Starting March 7 at 10 a.m. until March 9 at 8 p.m., the event is upgraded to the "All Out" version, offering additional perks. Incensed used during this period may lead to legendary Pokémon encounters if luck is on your side. Pikachu wearing hats modeled after trainers will appear in one-star raids. In five-star raids, Pokémon such as Articuno, Zapdos, Moltres, Deoxys (Normal Form), Darkrai, Genesect (No Drive), Nihilego, Buzzwole, Pheromosa, Xurkitree, Celesteela, Kartana, Guzzlord, Stakataka, Blacephalon, and Necrozma will be available.

Regional Ultra Beasts will only appear in their respective regions: Buzzwole in the Americas and Greenland, Pheromosa in Europe, the Middle East, Africa, and India, and Xurkitree in Asia-Pacific regions.

Baby Pokémon like Pichu, Cleffa, Igglybuff, Togepi, Tyrogue, Smoochum, Elekid, Magby, Azurill, Wynaut, Budew, Chingling, Bonsly, Mime Jr., Happiny, Munchlax, Riolu, Mantyke, and Toxel will hatch from 2 km eggs. Mime Jr. remains region-locked to Europe.

Most Pokémon released so far will be available in the wild, excluding baby Pokémon, costumed Pokémon, Ultra Beasts, some mythical Pokémon, and others such as Mewtwo, Smeargle, Shedinja, Kecleon, Giratina, Zoroark, Larvesta, Volcarona, Scatterbug, Spewpa, Vivillon, Zygarde, Mudbray, Mudsdale, Cosmog, Cosmoem, Morpeko, Eternatus, Kubfu, Urshifu, Maushold, Dudunsparce, Gimmighoul, and Gholdengo.
