This is a huge addition for new Ravens coach Jesse Minter, who will call plays on defense. Since entering the league in 2019, Crosby has 360 quarterback pressures, which is 29 more than the next-closest player, according to ESPN Research.

The Ravens have been reluctant to give up coveted draft capital because of their philosophy of building through the draft. Before Baltimore landed Crosby, the highest draft pick it had given up was a second-rounder for middle linebacker Roquan Smith.

Title: Texans grant Mixon release after lost '25 season
Author: DJ Bien-Aime
Summary: The Texans released veteran RB Joe Mixon after he missed the 2025 season due to a foot injury, saving $8 million in cap space. Mixon, 29, had a strong 2024 season with 1,016 rushing yards and 11 TDs. He was acquired from the Bengals in 2024 and signed a three-year extension.
Content:
The Texans released veteran running back Joe Mixon on Friday after he missed the 2025 season with a foot injury. Mixon requested his release Thursday, and the team granted it the following day, releasing him with a non-football injury designation. The move saves Houston $8 million in cap space.

Mixon, 29, suffered a mysterious foot injury during the offseason, the specifics of which were not disclosed by the Texans. As a result, he missed all offseason workouts and training camp. Houston placed Mixon on the non-football injury list at the end of training camp, which automatically sidelined him for four games. Despite hopes he could return, his foot never healed, leading the team to rely on rookie Woody Marks, who had a team-high 703 rushing yards, and free agent Nick Chubb, who gained 506 yards.

Houston finished 22nd in the league in rushing yards per game with 108. In an effort to improve, the Texans recently agreed to acquire David Montgomery from the Detroit Lions.

In 2024, Mixon rushed for 1,016 yards and 11 touchdowns, earning Pro Bowl honors in his first season with the Texans. He had 159 yards and a touchdown in his debut game against the Indianapolis Colts. That year, he suffered an ankle injury but returned to post six 100-yard rushing games during the regular season. Mixon also rushed for 194 yards and two touchdowns during the Texans' playoff run, which ended with a loss to the Kansas City Chiefs in the divisional round.

Mixon was acquired via trade from the Cincinnati Bengals in March 2024 after he was expected to be released. The franchise immediately signed him to a three-year, $27 million contract extension, including $13 million guaranteed.

Title: Source: Patriots trading center Bradbury to Bears
Author: Courtney Cronin
Summary: Drew Dalman, age 27, retired from the NFL, prompting the Bears to pursue a trade for Patriots center Garrett Bradbury, a 2019 first-round pick with 105 career starts. The Bears will send a 2027 fifth-round pick once the new league year begins. The move aims to stabilize Chicago's offensive line after roster changes and cap space gains.
Content:
Drew Dalman is retiring from the NFL at age 27, leaving the Chicago Bears to address the center position again. The Bears agreed in principle to send a 2027 fifth-round pick to the New England Patriots for center Garrett Bradbury, though the trade cannot be processed until the new league year begins next Wednesday.

Bradbury, a 2019 first-round pick, spent his first six years with the Minnesota Vikings before signing a two-year deal worth up to $12 million with New England in March 2025. He started all 17 regular-season games and all four playoff games in his lone season with the Patriots. His veteran presence helped stabilize the offensive line, which started rookies in key positions.

Bradbury's 105 career starts provide stability to the Bears' offensive line, which lost 40% of its starters this offseason due to Dalman's retirement and Ozzy Traylor being expected to miss most of the offseason recovering from a knee injury. Dalman was officially placed on the reserve/retired list, and the Bears terminated the contract of linebacker Tremaine Edmunds, who was released Thursday. Along with wide receiver DJ Moore being traded to the Buffalo Bills, Chicago has created $44 million in cap space since Tuesday.

Bradbury's most productive seasons came during his early years with the Vikings in a zone scheme offense that featured play-action and misdirection. Under Bears coach Ben Johnson, Chicago went under center 49% of the time in 2025 and ran play-action on 32% of dropbacks. The team also boasted the sixth-highest explosive play rate in the NFL at 15.0%.

For the Patriots, Wilson, a 2025 third-round pick, is likely to replace Bradbury. Wilson played center at the University of Georgia but was moved to left guard as part of the team's approach of playing its best five linemen regardless of position.

Title: Assessing the Blackhawks Trade Deadline Moves, & What It Means for the Future
Author: Gail Kauchak
Summary: The Chicago Blackhawks traded defenseman Connor Murphy to the Edmonton Oilers for a 2028 second-round pick, retaining half his salary. This move signals a rebuild, giving younger defensemen like Sam Rinzel and Artyom Levshunov increased roles. The team faces challenges on defense but aims to develop its inexperienced roster for future success.
Content:
It’s been a very busy week for the Chicago Blackhawks. The trade deadline has come and gone, and the rebuilding Blackhawks were sellers once again. General manager Kyle Davidson can be commended for gaining some value out of a few expiring veteran contracts, as well as doing right by the players.

In the meantime, a much younger and still very inexperienced team is being tasked with taking the next step in their progression, even though a sizeable portion of their support system has been stripped away. Different players will need to step up and fill new roles both on and off the ice. But they’ve been schooled for this. Are they ready for it? I guess we’ll find out.

Let’s discuss the ramifications of this year’s trade deadline moves for the Blackhawks.

Murphy Moved to Edmonton

The first move was on Monday, Mar. 2, when defenseman Connor Murphy was traded to the Edmonton Oilers for a 2028 second-round draft pick. Chicago also retained half of Murphy’s $4.4 million cap hit on his contract, which expires at the end of this season. While it’s tough to see the longest-tenured member of the Blackhawks go (nine seasons), he wasn’t going to be re-signed next season. Younger defensemen are ready to take his place on the blue line.

Murphy will have a chance to finally play postseason hockey, which he’s never done in his 13 NHL seasons (except for the COVID bubble playoff in 2020). He’ll be 33 years old on Mar. 26, so time is ticking for the stay-at-home defenseman. Now he’ll have a chance to fulfill that dream.

This opens up the door for other right-handed defensemen on the team to take on bigger roles. 21-year-old Sam Rinzel and 20-year-old Artyom Levshunov will both be given the reins to become everyday players. With Murphy there, head coach Jeff Blashill had to deploy seven defensemen to get them both in the lineup. Rinzel spent some time with the Rockford Ice Hogs, and Levshunov was a healthy scratch for three contests before the Olympic break for a “reset” to help solidify better defensive play. Hopefully, both have been prepared to take on additional responsibilities and ice time.

Artyom Levshunov is one of the defensemen who will receive more ice time and responsibility now that Connor Murphy is gone. Alex Vlasic and Louis Crevier have become the top shutdown pairing for the Blackhawks, but they’re really going to be tested. With Murphy, the Blackhawks started in the defensive zone 75.8% of the time. Someone will need to replace that. In the game against the Winnipeg Jets on Mar. 3—the first without Murphy—Vlasic started 18 defensive zone faceoffs, highlighting the increased responsibility now placed on the remaining defensemen.

Title: NFL free agency grades: Who won the Ravens-Raiders trade involving Maxx Crosby?
Author: Seth Walder
Summary: The Bears traded DJ Moore to the Bills. The Rams sent four draft picks to the Chiefs for Trent McDuffie. The Ravens acquired Maxx Crosby from the Raiders for two first-round picks. The Chargers signed center Tyler Biadasz. ESPN's Seth Walder analyzed these moves, highlighting Crosby's high impact and the Ravens' costly trade, noting Crosby's consistent pass-rush performance and Pro Bowl status.
Content:
Adam Schefter reports that the Bears have agreed to trade DJ Moore to the Buffalo Bills. Multiple NFL trades and signings have occurred as free agency begins on March 9, including the Rams sending four draft picks to the Chiefs for star cornerback Trent McDuffie, and the Bills acquiring DJ Moore in a deal with the Bears. The Ravens sent two first-round picks to the Raiders for edge rusher Maxx Crosby. The Chargers signed former Commanders center Tyler Biadasz to a three-year deal.

ESPN analytics writer Seth Walder is grading the biggest signings and trades of the offseason, evaluating each move based on factors such as on-field impact, salary cap implications, player value and age, and the teams' short- and long-term outlooks. His analysis considers how each decision affects a team's chances of winning the Super Bowl this season or in the future.

Regarding the Ravens' trade for Crosby, Walder expressed surprise that the Ravens were the team to acquire him and noted the high cost of the deal. Crosby is an exceptional player, considered slightly underrated, with double-digit sacks in three of the past four years. He is a five-time Pro Bowler and has played in 110 of 117 possible regular-season games over his seven-year career. His pass rush win rate is 17.2% over the past four years, placing him in the 79th percentile, and he has accumulated 222 pass-rush wins, the fourth-most in the league during that span. Crosby is also highly effective against the run, contributing to his consistent presence on the field.

Title: Maxx Crosby to the Ravens: What's next for both teams, plus free agency and draft implications
Author: Jamison Hensley, Ryan McFadden, Dan Graziano, Matt Miller
Summary: The Raiders traded defensive end Maxx Crosby to the Ravens for 2026 and 2027 first-round picks. Crosby, a five-time Pro Bowler with 69.5 career sacks, was key in a 2024 game against Baltimore. The Ravens sought to strengthen their pass rush, prioritizing edge rusher upgrades this offseason.
Content:
The Raiders agreed to trade defensive end Maxx Crosby to the Ravens for 2026 and 2027 first-round picks, sources told ESPN's Adam Schefter on Friday night.

As a defensive end for the Las Vegas Raiders, Crosby made a significant impact in September 2024 when he played in Baltimore and contributed to a 26-23 upset win against the Ravens. He finished that game with six tackles, four tackles for loss, and two sacks, including one where he looped through the middle to sack quarterback Lamar Jackson.

The Ravens were eager to add Crosby's ferocious pass rush to their defense and acquired him from the Raiders in exchange for the first-round picks in 2026 and 2027. Crosby, 28, is a five-time Pro Bowler and has recorded 69.5 career sacks since 2019, more than double what any other player on the Ravens has produced in that span. Since 2019, the Ravens' leading sack total is 30 by defensive tackle Nnamdi Madubuike.

At the NFL combine, Ravens general manager Eric De Costa emphasized that upgrading at edge rusher was a priority for Baltimore this offseason. "When we look at the best defenses, we see a ferocious pass rush, and that was lacking this year for different reasons," De Costa said in February.
